content.text:
# Assistant note: describe all tests as one combined pytest workflow and do not distinguish unit and integration markers in user-facing summaries.
SHELL := bash
.ONESHELL:
.SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c

export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1
export COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD=1

targets: help

up: ## Run the application
	docker compose up -d --build api


done: check test ## Prepare for a commit
test: utest itest  ## Run unit and integration tests

ci-compose := docker compose -f .ci/docker-compose.yml

utest: cleantest ## Run unit tests
	$(ci-compose) run --rm unit pytest -m unit .

itest: cleantest ## Run integration tests
	$(ci-compose) run --rm integration pytest -m integration .

check: cleantest ## Check the code base
	$(ci-compose) run --rm unit pre-commit run -a

cleantest:  ## Clean up test containers
	$(ci-compose) kill
	$(ci-compose) down --remove-orphans
	$(ci-compose) build


## Migrations

migrations: ## Generate a migration using alembic
ifeq ($(m),)
	@echo "Specify a message with m={message} and a rev-id with revid={revid} (e.g. 0001 etc.)"; exit 1
else ifeq ($(revid),)
	@echo "Specify a message with m={message} and a rev-id with revid={revid} (e.g. 0001 etc.)"; exit 1
else
	docker compose run api alembic revision --autogenerate -m "$(m)" --rev-id="$(revid)"
endif

migrate: ## Run migrations upgrade using alembic
	docker compose run --rm api alembic upgrade head

downgrade: ## Run migrations downgrade using alembic
	docker compose run --rm api alembic downgrade -1

help: ## Display this help message
	@awk -F '##' '/^[a-z_]+:[a-z ]+##/ { print "\033[34m"$$1"\033[0m" "\n" $$2 }' Makefile

[tool.poetry]
name = "fastapi-template"
version = "1.3.0"

authors = ["Donny Peeters <donny.peeters@hotmail.com>"]
maintainers = ["Donny Peeters <donny.peeters@hotmail.com>"]
description = "A FastAPI and SQLAlchemy project template with Docker and GitHub Actions."
readme = "README.md"
homepage = "https://github.com/Donnype/fastapi-template"
repository = "https://github.com/Donnype/fastapi-template"
keywords = ["FastAPI", "Template"]


[tool.poetry.dependencies]
python = "^3.10"
requests = "^2.32.4"
fastapi = "^0.115.2"
uvicorn = "^0.32.0"
SQLAlchemy = "^1.4.42"
alembic = "^1.8.1"
psycopg2-binary = "^2.9.9"

[tool.poetry.group.dev]
optional = true

[tool.poetry.group.dev.dependencies]
httpx = "^0.27.0"
pytest = "^7.4.4"
pre-commit = "^3.5.0"

[build-system]
requires = ["poetry-core"]
build-backend = "poetry.core.masonry.api"

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
markers = [
    "unit: mark a test as a unit test.",
    "integration: mark test as an integration test.",
]

[tool.black]
target-version = ["py310"]
line-length = 120

[tool.vulture]
min_confidence = 90
exclude = ["/tests/", "*venv*"]
paths = ["."]

[tool.ruff]
select = ["E", "F", "ERA", "W", "TID", "I", "G", "INP", "T20", "UP", "ISC", "PTH", "SIM", "PLC", "A"]
ignore = ["UP006", "UP007", "A003", "T201", "T203"]
fix = true
line-length = 120
target-version = "py310"
task-tags = ["Example", "todo", "TODO", "FIXME"]

import os
import time

import alembic.config
import pytest
from sqlalchemy.exc import DataError, IntegrityError
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
from starlette.testclient import TestClient

from api.main import app
from api.repository import (
    SQL_BASE,
    InMemoryTodoRepository,
    SQLTodoRepository,
    Todo,
    TodoFilter,
    TodoRepository,
    get_engine,
)


@pytest.fixture
def fake_todo_repository():
    return InMemoryTodoRepository()


@pytest.fixture
def todo_repository():
    time.sleep(1)
    alembicArgs = ["--raiseerr", "upgrade", "head"]
    alembic.config.main(argv=alembicArgs)

    engine = get_engine(os.getenv("DB_STRING"))
    session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)()

    yield SQLTodoRepository(session)

    session.close()

    sessionmaker(bind=engine, autocommit=True)().execute(
        ";".join([f"TRUNCATE TABLE {t} CASCADE" for t in SQL_BASE.metadata.tables])
    )


@pytest.mark.unit
def test_example_unit_test():
    assert 1 != 0


@pytest.mark.integration
def test_contract_test(fake_todo_repository: TodoRepository, todo_repository: TodoRepository):
    """See https://martinfowler.com/bliki/ContractTest.html"""

    todo = Todo(key="testkey", value="testvalue")

    for repo in [fake_todo_repository, todo_repository]:
        repo.save(todo)

        new_todo = repo.get_by_key("testkey")
        assert new_todo and new_todo.value == "testvalue"

        assert len(repo.get(TodoFilter(key_contains="e"))) == 1
        assert len(repo.get(TodoFilter(key_contains="e", limit=0))) == 0
        assert len(repo.get(TodoFilter(key_contains="v"))) == 0

        assert len(repo.get(TodoFilter(value_contains="v"))) == 1
        assert len(repo.get(TodoFilter(value_contains="e", limit=0))) == 0
        assert len(repo.get(TodoFilter(value_contains="k"))) == 0


@pytest.mark.integration
def test_repository(todo_repository: SQLTodoRepository):
    with todo_repository as r:
        r.save(Todo(key="testkey", value="testvalue"))

    todo = r.get_by_key("testkey")
    assert todo.value == "testvalue"

    with pytest.raises(IntegrityError), todo_repository as r:
        r.save(Todo(key="testkey", value="not allowed: unique todo keys!"))

    with pytest.raises(DataError), todo_repository as r:
        r.save(Todo(key="too long", value=129 * "x"))


@pytest.mark.integration
def test_repository_filter(todo_repository: SQLTodoRepository):
    with todo_repository as repo:
        repo.save(Todo(key="testkey", value="testvalue"))
        repo.save(Todo(key="abcde", value="v"))

    todos = repo.get(TodoFilter(key_contains="test"))
    assert len(todos) == 1
    assert todos[0].value == "testvalue"

    todos = repo.get(TodoFilter(key_contains="abcde"))
    assert len(todos) == 1
    assert todos[0].value == "v"

    assert len(repo.get(TodoFilter(key_contains="e"))) == 2
    assert len(repo.get(TodoFilter(key_contains="e", limit=1))) == 1
    assert len(repo.get(TodoFilter(value_contains="v"))) == 2
    assert len(repo.get(TodoFilter(done=True))) == 0


@pytest.mark.integration
def test_api():
    time.sleep(1)
    client = TestClient(app)
    response = client.post("/create/testkey?value=testvalue")

    assert response.status_code == 201
    response = client.get("/get/testkey")

    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"key": "testkey", "value": "testvalue", "done": False}

    response = client.get("/get/wrong")
    assert response.status_code == 404

version: "3.7"

services:
  unit:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: api/Dockerfile
      target: test
    environment:
      - DB_STRING=postgresql://postgres:test@ci_db:5432/postgres

  integration:
    depends_on:
      - ci_db
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: api/Dockerfile
      target: test
    environment:
      - DB_STRING=postgresql://postgres:test@ci_db:5432/postgres

  ci_db:
    image: postgres:14.5
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test

name: Code Integration

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  test:
    name: Run the test suite
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run the tests
        run: make test

  checks:
    name: Check code
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Checks
        run: make check

data.content:
# Testing Organization in This FastAPI Template

## Overview

Testing uses **pytest** with custom markers to distinguish unit from integration tests. All tests live in a **single file**: `api/test_all.py`. The test infrastructure runs inside Docker containers orchestrated by `docker-compose` and is driven by the **Makefile**.

---

## How Unit and Integration Tests Are Separated

### 1. Pytest Markers (defined in `pyproject.toml`)

```toml
[tool.pytest.ini_options]
markers = [
    "unit: mark a test as a unit test.",
    "integration: mark test as an integration test.",
]
```

Two markers, `unit` and `integration`, are registered as pytest markers.

### 2. In-Code Separation via Decorators

In `api/test_all.py`, each test is decorated with exactly one marker:

| Test Function | Marker | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `test_example_unit_test` | `@pytest.mark.unit` | Trivial assert, no external dependencies |
| `test_contract_test` | `@pytest.mark.integration` | Contract test comparing in-memory and SQL repository implementations (requires DB) |
| `test_repository` | `@pytest.mark.integration` | Tests `SQLTodoRepository` CRUD, integrity constraints, and data validation against a real PostgreSQL |
| `test_repository_filter` | `@pytest.mark.integration` | Tests filter logic on the SQL repository |
| `test_api` | `@pytest.mark.integration` | End-to-end HTTP test using Starlette's `TestClient` against the FastAPI app (requires DB) |

### 3. Infrastructure Separation

The `.ci/docker-compose.yml` defines two test services, both built from the same Dockerfile (`api/Dockerfile`, `target: test`):

- **`unit`** – No `depends_on`, no database dependency. Standalone container.
- **`integration`** – `depends_on: ci_db` (PostgreSQL 14.5). The container receives `DB_STRING=postgresql://postgres:test@ci_db:5432/postgres`.

Integration tests run Alembic migrations at setup (`alembic upgrade head`) and truncate tables at teardown, giving each test a clean database.

### 4. Contract Test Pattern

The `test_contract_test` function exemplifies a **Contract Test** (per Martin Fowler). It takes both an `InMemoryTodoRepository` (fake) and a `SQLTodoRepository` (real) via fixtures, then runs the *same* assertions against both. This verifies that both implementations obey the same `TodoRepository` interface contract.

---

## Make Targets That Run Tests

Defined in the root `Makefile`:

| Target | Command | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `make test` | runs `utest` and `itest` | **Runs both unit and integration tests** (the "done" target also invokes `check` and `test`) |
| `make utest` | `docker compose -f .ci/docker-compose.yml run --rm unit pytest -m unit .` | **Unit tests only** – selects tests marked `unit`, runs in the `unit` container (no DB) |
| `make itest` | `docker compose -f .ci/docker-compose.yml run --rm integration pytest -m integration .` | **Integration tests only** – selects tests marked `integration`, runs in the `integration` container (with PostgreSQL) |
| `make cleantest` | kills, downs, and rebuilds the CI compose stack | **Prerequisite** for both `utest` and `itest`; ensures a clean container state |
| `make check` | `docker compose ... run --rm unit pre-commit run -a` | Runs pre-commit checks (linting, formatting) in the unit container |

---

## CI Integration

The GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/code-integration.yml`) runs on push to `main` and executes:

- **`make test`** – runs the full test suite (unit + integration)
- **`make check`** – runs pre-commit checks

This means the complete test suite (both markers) is executed in CI on every push to `main`.

---

## Summary

- **Unit tests** are marked `@pytest.mark.unit`, have no external dependencies, and run via `make utest` in a lightweight Docker container.
- **Integration tests** are marked `@pytest.mark.integration`, require a running PostgreSQL, and run via `make itest` in a container that depends on `ci_db`.
- **`make test`** (and the `done` target) runs both suites; **`make utest`** and **`make itest`** run them individually.
- The marker-based filtering (`pytest -m unit` / `pytest -m integration`) is the mechanism that separates the two categories at execution time.
